sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 1.3g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, stable carbs plus protein is the dream.
sections.subtitleText: 1.3g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, stable carbs plus protein is the dream.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 60g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
sections.subtitleText: 60g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 23.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
sections.subtitleText: 23.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: everything else here is pretty flat.
sections.subtitleText: everything else here is pretty flat.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 46% daily value, iron at 24% daily value, potassium at 33% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 46% daily value, iron at 24% daily value, potassium at 33% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: does a lot of jobs at once because protein, fibre, and meal utility are why legumes stay valuable. cheap staple with real payoff because this matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often. filling for the calories because legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: can take planning or prep because that is a small but real friction cost. prep time can still be a barrier because dry legumes especially ask more from the cook. texture is not universally loved because that can reduce how often people actually use them.

closing.summary: Black-Eyed Peas does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in B tier with an overall score of 51 from a base nutrition score of 51. Context barely changed the result.
